Review Updated: My late father hired Ms. Toombs about a month before he passed away. She prepared all the necessary legal documents (such as his will, health care proxy, and power of attorney) for a reasonable fee. She also handled the closing on our family’s old home, also at a fair rate.

My earlier negative review was based on a misunderstanding caused by a former employee of Ms. Toombs, named Pat, who contacted our family after leaving the office. Unfortunately, we trusted her and allowed her to take over POA responsibilities, but in hindsight, we felt this was a mistake and deeply regretted not staying with Ms. Toombs from the start. That situation caused a lot of confusion and stress for our family.

We’re grateful that Ms. Toombs was professional and understanding when I contacted her to straighten things out. Overall, she was a good elder care attorney for our family. Since English is not my mother's first language and father had done everything for her (so she had a big learning curve), I help support her in communication and decision-making. Thank you again, Ms. Toombs.
reviews.ownerResponse: Although you may have been disappointed, your mother was the client, not you.  Your mother's instructions to me were paramount and were followed.  I am not required to report to, and in fact, ethics rules prohibit interaction with family when their interests are not what the client wants, or to counsel family members who are dissatisfied with a parent's choices.  Neither I, or any of my staff, are a social worker, medical professional or personal care managers.

I will preface by stating that this is the second time I have hired MK. The first was about 10 years ago. At that time, he was spot on, very organized and a very decent lawyer. I believe making law guardian of the year at one point. Unfortunately, the family court system I believe has burnt him out. Although he has always appeared on time for court appearances, he comes very disorganized. He is a total mess in court and gets frustrated very easily if other attorneys say something that he disagrees with. Very unprofessional. At one point, the law guardian in my case made an accusation that MK was just taking my money. I later found out that is exactly what was happening. We met several times for pre-trial and MK made many grand statements and promises that gave me hope. He was going to subpoena witnesses, get the law guardian removed, ask for a Lincoln hearing and even get the evidence from the other side dismissed because they missed the submission date by a month.  The day of the trial came and everything that we talked about went up in smoke. We never even went in for trial. It is important to note that I only paid him an additional $1K (on top of $2K initial retainer) because he promised that "win or lose, I'm taking this through trial". Well, he took my trial money and folded like a cheap tent the day of the trial. At one point telling me behind closed doors that if I took a certain offer now, he would come back and represent me for free in a year or two. Can anyone say quid pro quo? In addition, he stumbled and bumbled so badly in the court room that my order  doesn't even reflect what was agreed upon. Even the other party agrees! Now I have to go back and try to fix the mess myself.
I would not hire MK ever again. My case is so badly messed up that I don't even want him to try to fix it. I will take my chances doing it myself.

reviews.text: I am very pleased with the counsel provided by Juda Law Office. I have been a client for years and have never been dissatisfied or misled. I trust Juda Law wholeheartedly and recommend their services whenever I can.
Help me win every case thank mark

I know Mark Juda from Schenectady Rotary Club where he is a diligent volunteer. Recently I had a traffic situation. Mark Juda to the rescue! I found him to be thoroughly conscientious, accessible and professional in handling my case. Because of Mark and his attentive staff, my squeaky clean driving record remains so.  Some folks just plead guilty and are faced with “points” on their driving record which means a hefty fine and increased insurance fees. I strongly advocate  getting legal assistance; especially from Juda Law Office PPLC.  Mark’s service area is not limited to Schenectady cases. My situation was settled through the Saratoga County ADA’s office. One more thing, Mark is not an intimidating person. He has a warm and friendly personality and enjoyable sense of humor which he can turn off when he needs to get down to business. I am so happy that I turned to Mark Juda. Sincerely, JMD, a female who will celebrate her 82nd birthday in July.

Mark Juda is the BEST! He is competent and compassionate and will ALWAYS keep it real when discussing the matter he is representing! I will forever be indebted to Mark Juda!!

Mark and Kim were instrumental in closing on our first two home purchases and we couldn’t be happier with the attention to detail, customer service, and overall just providing a great experience on both occasions. I was arrested and wrongfully accused of something and at the time my wife was scared to death not knowing what to do she reached out to marks office and him and his secretary talked a really good story mark came down to the county jail to speak with me and I shoulda known then he was a joke because my consultation lasted 5 mins he was barely listening and more concerned about his 5,000 retainer now I haven’t been in trouble since I was a good so I didn’t know what lawyers had good reputations at the time and man I wish I did because I was wrongfully convicted and now have a life sentence and spent 4 1/2 years in prison with the evidence and medical paper work this case should have been dismissed with the right attorney I asked mark to do several things like phone records and what not to prove my innocence and he did nothing! Every court appearance he wouldn’t speak up no matter what I was denied bail twice because he wouldn’t speak up and let the DA and judge walk all over him so to make the story short I was pretty much forced to plead guilty to not chance having to go to trial with a lawyer that was doing nothing for me now all these local lawyers work with these prosecutors they play golf together etc do your research before you hire this man because I spent 5,000 to have to plead guilty and now I am fighting for my life back and working with the innocent project because this man did nothing specially when all so called evidence was in my favor proving my innocence he didn’t even put in a motion for dismissal due to lack of evidence or the medical examiner papers proving my innocence with all DNA also coming back in my favor all the motions he did put in were all denied due to errors he made in the motions. This is hard topic for me and my family but thought I would post a real review to hopefully save someone else’s life and families also don’t let him or his secretary fool you when they always talk about he was the first lawyer in the capital district to beat a murder case which is totally false because he was representing one of the defendants and Cheryl Coleman was representing the other and Cheryl Coleman beat that case he didn’t

My rating is based on his poor customer service, lack of commitment or communication and lack of knowledge regarding what the court office made seem like common knowledge.
I've called twice  in three months still no response.

I called on behalf of my son. my son has a public defender. But, hes want outside lawyer. I explain to lady on phone on 2/10/2022. I told her twice my son has been unable to speak to anybody inside. And  he needs to speak to a lawyer and I dont have much knowledge of his case..She proceeds to direct me to call a public conflict defender. That was her way of helping me. Her words..She seem very Bias.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Shalena,

I'm so sorry that this is how you interpreted what was suggested to you. You stated that your son was being held in the Schenectady County Jail and should have been released a long time ago as the matter was resolved. You were concerned that he was supposed to be moved to a federal prison and that hadn't happened yet. As you mentioned your son had received assigned counsel services most likely from the Public Defender or Conflict Defender, it was suggested to you that you speak to the attorney that represented him as they would be the person who would know the most about the situation. As you had no information at all about any of you son's charges, it made the most sense that you work with the attorney that had represented your son on these matters. I apologize if you didn't understand that you were only being told this because it actually would be the most beneficial thing for you to do. You never mentioned wanting to retain the services of this firm. Please feel free to give us a call back if there is something we can help you with.
